title: Automations for Specific Item
excerpt:
In my organisation we use template pages for multiple projects. 
Am I able to assign automations for specific items? (Notify specific person, even when they are not assigned to task, when item X is complete)

posts.author: ScottMuirZapi (Scott Muir)
posts.cookedHtml:
In my organisation we use template pages for multiple projects.

Am I able to assign automations for specific items? (Notify specific person, even when they are not assigned to task, when item X is complete)

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Scott!

It’s not possible to specify automations per item but you could set up another status column, name it after the person or item, and then specifically only manually trigger it when you want it to trigger the action for that item.

Does this make sense? Let me know if I didn’t quite explain it well!

It’s not the best workaround but hopefully it could help?

posts.author: ScottMuirZapi (Scott Muir)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you, this has worked absolutely how I need!

I have made another status column which is simply Yes / No and hidden from the rest of the team.

This also allows us to change which ones automatically e-mail the specific team member.

Thanks again

posts.author: allanfeldman (Allan Feldman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, we need a similar automation for what we do. We want to assign automations for specific items to notify a specific person whenever a specific custom status label is marked.

posts.author: josuexvolt (Josh Cruz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

I’m Josh Cruz, a Project Manager at DeltaPMO. We’re a project management consultancy firm and proud partners with Monday.com.

Guess what? We have joined forces with another innovative company and we are currently developing a brand new app for Monday.com. This app is designed to cater to your needs with a variety of recipes, actions, and triggers.

Imagine this: “If item A changes, item B changes too!” That’s the core workflow of our app.

We’d love to hear your thoughts on this upcoming app. Do you think it could be a game-changer for Monday.com users like you? Could it solve your problems or boost your workflows?

We’ve attached some screenshots below to give you a sneak peek of an example recipe and how it would appear in our app. Your feedback would mean the world to us.

And hey, if you’re as excited as we are and want to be one of the first to try it out, you can sign up HERE for our waitlist and get early access.

Looking forward to see some replies!

Best Wishes, Josh Cruz

posts.author: Trevster (Trevor Lawson)
posts.cookedHtml: That level of granularity would be very useful.

posts.author: CatUiNeill (Cat Uí Néill)
posts.cookedHtml:
HI @ScottMuirZapi A feature request has been submitted to address this issue:

Item-Specific Automation Trigger Feature requests
Dear Monday.com Team, I hope this message finds you well! I am writing to suggest a new feature that could significantly enhance the functionality and precision of Monday.com automations: Item-Specific Automation Triggers. Currently, automations in Monday.com work well at a board-wide level, but there is no option to create automations tied exclusively to a single item. For example, I’d like to set up an automation that triggers only when a specific task (e.g., “Finalize Budget Report”) change…

I’m hoping that if it gets enough votes Monday will design/implement it.
